why_this_milestone_exists.purpose: Record the second Kangaroo smoke run and the new failure mode discovered after adding Kangaroo logger feedback.
why_this_milestone_exists.trigger: The second short Kangaroo run completed with two LLM iterations and saved LLM input/output artifacts, but still scored 0.0.

interpretation: The second run shows that the LLM understood the UP-only diagnosis but overcorrected into a horizontal-only policy.
Policy v1 was still mostly UP and scored 0.0. Policy v2 alternated LEFT/RIGHT near the start, never climbed, scored 0.0, and lost one life.
Kangaroo remains unsolved. The pipeline, LLM audit logging, diagnosis prompt, policy rewrite path, and logger metrics are working, but the game-specific route-control context is still insufficient.

code_changes_after_diagnosis.change: Added prompt and feedback warnings against horizontal-only Kangaroo dithering.
Added support for rendering a specific optimization history entry, enabling policy_v2 GIF output.

rq_relevance.RQ1: Negative evidence for Kangaroo so far: two smoke attempts completed but no successful policy yet.
rq_relevance.RQ2: The failure classification is useful: score alone hid whether failure was UP-only or horizontal-only.

next_steps.step: Do not launch a full 10000-step Kangaroo run yet.
next_steps.why: Both short attempts score 0.0 and expose basic route-control failures.

next_steps.step: Improve Kangaroo context around ladder reachability: when to climb, when to traverse, and how to target the next ladder after reaching a platform.
next_steps.why: The failures are not from syntax or API plumbing; they are from insufficient route-mode logic.

next_steps.step: Run a third short smoke only after the prompt explicitly describes climb-band entry, platform traversal, and anti-dither conditions.
next_steps.why: This keeps API spending focused on fixing the diagnosed failure modes.
